items.briefing:
Phishing attacks are becoming more sophisticated and frequent, with AI now being used to craft highly convincing fake emails at scale — making them harder to spot than ever. Separately, a Chinese cybercrime group known as TA4922 is expanding its attacks globally, which may increase risk for Australian organisations. Australian businesses are also among the world's highest users of self-hosted AI tools, which can introduce security risks if those systems aren't properly managed or updated.

**Practical tip:** Be extra cautious with unexpected emails asking you to click links or provide information — even if they look legitimate, verify directly with the sender before acting.

featured.title: Australia leads in self-hosted AI use, JFrog finds
featured.quote: Australia's AI adoption leadership creates security gaps in secrets scanning and audit capabilities.
